texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Supports documentation of a patient's vital signs (e.g., temperature, pulse, and respiration).
texts.text: Supports documentation of a patient's vital signs (e.g., temperature, pulse, and respiration).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Tracks a patient's height, weight, central venous pressure (CVP), circumference/girth and oxygen saturation via oximetry with supplemental oxygen information.
texts.text: Tracks a patient's height, weight, central venous pressure (CVP), circumference/girth and oxygen saturation via oximetry with supplemental oxygen information.

texts.label: list_item

texts.orig: An ―application window‖ is the area on your computer screen used by a program.  If you have more than one program running at the same time, you can go from one program to another by clicking in each application window. The currently active window contains a colored bar (usually blue) at the top of the window. An inactive window contains a gray bar at the top of the window. You can also move, close, or minimize the application window to make room for another window. (See Help in Windows for further instructions on these functions.)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Some sites prefer to delay implementation of the software until they have a point of care data entry system, but this software can be implemented without a point of care system. Vital sign entry can be accomplished by ancillary service personnel, (e.g., PIMS, Dietetics, Pharmacy). Interested users of this software are encouraged to form a committee to work cooperatively on the implementation and training of the package. Setting up test wards is a good way to begin a cooperative implementation effort. The Vitals/Measurements module is appropriate for all personnel who obtain and record patient vitals/measurements. Conceivably this module could be used by nursing, dietetics, medicine, and other disciplines as appropriate.

texts.orig: Vitals/Measurements uses RPC Broker and VA FileMan Delphi Components in the display and navigation of screens. Vitals utilizes only the standard components as supplied with Delphi.
texts.text: Vitals/Measurements uses RPC Broker and VA FileMan Delphi Components in the display and navigation of screens. Vitals utilizes only the standard components as supplied with Delphi.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Below is a list of the Delphi components this application currently uses along with a short description.
texts.text: Below is a list of the Delphi components this application currently uses along with a short description.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: TeeChart	Displays charts and graphs. It is used in Vitals/Measurements to graphically plot various measurements versus time.
texts.text: TeeChart	Displays charts and graphs. It is used in Vitals/Measurements to graphically plot various measurements versus time.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: ResizerPanel	Resizes its client components when the form is resized or the screen resolution is changed. This takes care of proper size and position of components with regard to the font size used in Windows. It is there so users can resize the application Windows to meet their needs.
texts.text: ResizerPanel	Resizes its client components when the form is resized or the screen resolution is changed. This takes care of proper size and position of components with regard to the font size used in Windows. It is there so users can resize the application Windows to meet their needs.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: VersionInfoResource	Retrieves VERSIONINFO data from the executable. It is used in the about boxes in Vitals/Measurements to display version information.
texts.text: VersionInfoResource	Retrieves VERSIONINFO data from the executable. It is used in the about boxes in Vitals/Measurements to display version information.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: RPCBroker	Used for all non-FTP communication with the server.
texts.text: RPCBroker	Used for all non-FTP communication with the server.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: FMDC	Used for saving, deleting, validating, and retrieving data in FileMan data dictionaries.
texts.text: FMDC	Used for saving, deleting, validating, and retrieving data in FileMan data dictionaries.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: DateTime	Allows the user to visually select a date and time. It is provided as an option on all date/time fields.
texts.text: DateTime	Allows the user to visually select a date and time. It is provided as an option on all date/time fields.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: PatientSelectionFrame Allows user to select a patient, by unit, team, ward, clinic or name. The frame is on a resize panel.
texts.text: PatientSelectionFrame Allows user to select a patient, by unit, team, ward, clinic or name. The frame is on a resize panel.

texts.text: GMRVDATA has the following data: piece1^piece2^piece3^piece4^piece5

texts.label: text
texts.orig: piece1 = date/time in FileMan internal format piece2 = patient number from FILE 2 (i.e., DFN)
texts.text: piece1 = date/time in FileMan internal format piece2 = patient number from FILE 2 (i.e., DFN)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: piece3 = vital type, a semi-colon, the reading, a semi-colon, and oxygen flow rate and percentage values [optional] (e.g., 21;99;1 l/min 90%)
texts.text: piece3 = vital type, a semi-colon, the reading, a semi-colon, and oxygen flow rate and percentage values [optional] (e.g., 21;99;1 l/min 90%)

texts.label: text

texts.text: piece5 = user number from FILE 200 (i.e., DUZ), an asterisk, and the qualifier (File 120.52) internal entry numbers separated by colons (e.g., 547*50:65)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This Remote Procedure Call (RPC) performs various actions focusing on the user. The entry point is RPC^GMVRPCU. It has input parameters of RESULTS, OPTION and DATA (e.g., RPC^GMVRPCU(RESULTS,OPTION,DATA)).
texts.text: This Remote Procedure Call (RPC) performs various actions focusing on the user. The entry point is RPC^GMVRPCU. It has input parameters of RESULTS, OPTION and DATA (e.g., RPC^GMVRPCU(RESULTS,OPTION,DATA)).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The RESULTS variable contains the results of the call or the location where the results can be found.
texts.text: The RESULTS variable contains the results of the call or the location where the results can be found.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The OPTION variable identifies another entry point in the GMVRPCU routine that is invoked to process the call.
texts.text: The OPTION variable identifies another entry point in the GMVRPCU routine that is invoked to process the call.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The DATA variable contains any values needed by the OPTION variable to process the call.
texts.text: The DATA variable contains any values needed by the OPTION variable to process the call.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: When the OPTION value is SETPAR, this RPC will set and/or delete the value of a GMV USER DEFAULTS setting (e.g., the user's default template).
texts.text: When the OPTION value is SETPAR, this RPC will set and/or delete the value of a GMV USER DEFAULTS setting (e.g., the user's default template).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The DATA value is a two part value separated by a caret. The first part is name of a setting. The second part is the value of the setting. If the second part is null, the existing value of the setting is deleted.
texts.text: The DATA value is a two part value separated by a caret. The first part is name of a setting. The second part is the value of the setting. If the second part is null, the existing value of the setting is deleted.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If an error is encountered, a "-1" followed by a caret and the error message text (i.e., -1^error message) is returned.
texts.text: If an error is encountered, a "-1" followed by a caret and the error message text (i.e., -1^error message) is returned.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: When the OPTION value is GETPAR, this RPC will return the value of the GMV USER DEFAULTS setting specified in the DATA value.
texts.text: When the OPTION value is GETPAR, this RPC will return the value of the GMV USER DEFAULTS setting specified in the DATA value.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The DATA value is a one part value. It is the name of a setting (e.g., the user's default template).
texts.text: The DATA value is a one part value. It is the name of a setting (e.g., the user's default template).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: When the OPTION value is SIGNON, this RPC will return information about the user who is currently signed onto the system.
texts.text: When the OPTION value is SIGNON, this RPC will return information about the user who is currently signed onto the system.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The DATA value is not used. The user's IEN (i.e., DUZ) to the NEW PERSON (#200) file value must be defined when this call is made.
texts.text: The DATA value is not used. The user's IEN (i.e., DUZ) to the NEW PERSON (#200) file value must be defined when this call is made.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The RESULT variable will return the following array: RESULT(0)=NEW PERSON (#200) file internal entry number (DUZ) RESULT(1)=User's name (FILE 200, Field .01)
texts.text: The RESULT variable will return the following array: RESULT(0)=NEW PERSON (#200) file internal entry number (DUZ) RESULT(1)=User's name (FILE 200, Field .01)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: RESULT(2)=Domain (FILE 4.2) internal entry number RESULT(3)=Domain name (FILE 4.2, Field .01)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: RESULT(4)=Institution (FILE 4) internal entry number the user is signed into (i.e., DUZ(2))
texts.text: RESULT(4)=Institution (FILE 4) internal entry number the user is signed into (i.e., DUZ(2))

texts.label: text
texts.orig: RESULT(6)="0" or "1". "1" indicates the user has the GMV MANAGER or programmer key. "0" indicates the user has neither key.
texts.text: RESULT(6)="0" or "1". "1" indicates the user has the GMV MANAGER or programmer key. "0" indicates the user has neither key.

texts.label: text

texts.label: text
texts.orig: RESULT(9)=Number of seconds the system will wait for a response from the user (i.e., DTIME). The default time is 300 seconds.

User cannot set this value.
